segments.text: Sing for joy to God our strength; shout aloud to the God of Jacob!
Begin the music, strike the timbrel, play the melodious harp and lyre.
Sound the ram's horn at the New Moon, and when the moon is full, on the day of our festival;
this is a decree for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
When God went out against Egypt, he established it as a statute for Joseph. I heard an unknown voice say:
I removed the burden from their shoulders; their hands were set free from the basket.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In your distress you called and I rescued you, I answered you out of a thundercloud; I tested you at the waters of Meribah.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hear me, my people, and I will warn you— if you would only listen to me, Israel!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You shall have no foreign god among you; you shall not worship any god other than me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am the Lord your God, who brought you up out of Egypt. Open wide your mouth and I will fill it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But my people would not listen to me; Israel would not submit to me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts to follow their own devices.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If my people would only listen to me, if Israel would only follow my ways,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: how quickly I would subdue their enemies and turn my hand against their foes!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Those who hate the Lord would cringe before him, and their punishment would last forever.
But you would be fed with the finest of wheat; with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.
segments.speaker: God
